title: BlackBerry Stock Jump: QNX and Earnings Drive Turnaround
text: BlackBerry Limited (NYSE: BB) surged 4.1% on May 8, 2026, following strong quarterly results and positive momentum in its QNX operating system division. The company delivered impressive Q4 performance, with adjusted earnings per share of $0.06 beating expectations of $0.04, while revenue reached $156 million versus the anticipated $144.6 million. BlackBerry's fiscal year 2026 marked a turning point, as revenue increased 3% annually and 10% in the final quarter—reversing years of decline. QNX, BlackBerry's flagship software platform, achieved record revenue with 20% year-over-year growth in Q4 and 14% for the full year. The unit maintains a $950M royalty backlog providing visibility into future contracted revenue. The platform secured major automotive wins with Mercedes-Benz, BMW, and Volvo, plus a design win for Leapmotor's premium electric SUV entering mass production in 2026. Strategic defense collaborations with naval firm TKMS expand QNX's reach into next-generation platforms. Management provided FY27 revenue guidance of $584-611 million and non-GAAP EPS of $0.15-0.19. The company projects mid-single to low-double-digit revenue growth with approximately $100 million in operating cash flow. While fundamentals strengthened, valuation remains premium with a P/E ratio near 74.
